title: Is intrauterine exposure to acetaminophen associated with emotional and hyperactivity problems during childhood? Findings from the 2004 Pelotas birth cohort.
ehr_evidence: The study uses data from the 2004 Pelotas birth cohort, not EHR.
summary: A prospective longitudinal study investigating the association between intrauterine acetaminophen exposure and emotional and hyperactivity problems in children at ages 6 and 11 in a Brazilian birth cohort.
key_findings: Intrauterine acetaminophen exposure increased the odds of emotional problems (OR=1.47) and hyperactivity/inattention (OR=1.42) in 6-year-old boys.
No association was observed for girls.
The effect size decreased slightly at age 11.
study_design: Prospective longitudinal population-based birth cohort study
